meta.title: NAAC Test Audit — Recipe Surface + Smell Hunt
meta.description: Full-fidelity classification of every test/demo/baseline just recipe and every test smell across tests/. Excluding harness.

props.title: NAAC Test Surface Audit
props.subtitle: Where the tests lie, where 'demo' lost its meaning, and where the production pathway gets mocked away
props.meta: Scope: every test/demo/baseline just recipe + tests/ tree (excluding harness/)

props.content: Two complaints, one audit. (1) A class of 'faster tests' that became 'I'm going to fuck your shit up and lie.' (2) The word 'demo' now means five different things. Below: every recipe classified, every test smell ranked, and the three issue classes mapped to evidence.

props.title: FINDING 1 — Five files lie about production
props.content: Top 5 mock-heavy unit tests carry ~80% of all mock load. The worst patches modules.intent.daemon.create_broker AND DispatchEngine.from_config — so a real wiring regression doesn't flip the test red. These are the 'faster tests that lie'.

props.title: FINDING 2 — 'demo' means five different things
props.content: test-demo (integration test), demo-dump (scoring tool), baseline-demo (scorecard wrapper), gate-demo-mode-prod (profile guardrail), voice-demo-up (runtime stack). Same anti-pattern that produced the baseline-* sprawl already pinned in CLAUDE.md.

props.title: FINDING 3 — Docker test lane is vestigial
props.content: test-nats-e2e / test-audio-e2e / test-golden all spin Docker NATS. FakeDigiRig adapter (tests/integration/helpers/) already gives you the right surface. Docker lane predates that adoption. Collapse it.

props.label: PART 1A — RECIPES
props.title: Real, value-based tests (KEEP)
props.subtitle: These resolve to actual pytest runs against production code paths.

props.label: PART 1B — RECIPES
props.title: The 'demo' word means five different things
props.subtitle: User flag #2 confirmed. Same anti-pattern as baseline-* sprawl.

rows.does: grep demo_mode: true in prod profiles

props.label: PART 1C — RECIPES
props.title: Docker-NATS lane (USER FLAG #1 confirmed)
props.subtitle: These spin Docker NATS for tests. FakeDigiRig adapter exists; this lane is vestigial.

rows.does: docker compose up -d nats; sleep 3; pytest
docker compose --build + --wait test-runner for 10 ATC clips

props.title: Why the Docker lane should die
props.content: FakeDigiRig in tests/integration/helpers/ feeds audio IN through real adapters — exactly the boundary you want for E2E. The Docker lane was the way before that helper landed. Now it just adds 30s of compose orchestration per CI run for tests the FakeDigiRig path covers deterministically.

props.label: PART 1D — RECIPES
props.title: Not tests at all (RELABEL)
props.subtitle: The 'test-' prefix has been overloaded to mean 'anything I run before merging'.

rows.recipe: baseline / baseline-pe / baseline-pe-full / baseline-grounded / baseline-bulk / baseline-ci / baseline-demo (×7)

props.label: of mock load in 5 unit files
PART 2 — HOTLIST
props.title: Top lie candidates (priority order)
props.subtitle: These tests can pass while production breaks. Hand-inspect first.

rows.smell: patches modules.intent.daemon.create_broker AND DispatchEngine.from_config — decision gate runs against fake dispatch
tests the engine itself with 27 mocks; if patches replace internal handlers, routing isn't exercised
state-machine tests are common gutting sites; 750 lines — verify assertions exercise real transitions
line 177 sets rc.gate.allow_transcript_injection = False on MagicMock RuntimeConfig — recorder may not run real path

props.label: PART 2 — NOT SMELLS
props.title: Acceptable mock concentration
props.subtitle: High mock count alone isn't a smell. These are doing it right.

rows.why: Mocks shutil.which + subprocess.run for ffmpeg detection — INFRA mocking, exactly what the rule allows
both legit network injection — only mocks in 120+ integration files

props.label: PART 2 — UNEXPECTED
props.title: Surprising clean signals
props.subtitle: Things I expected to find that aren't there. Corrects my own pre-scan flags.

props.title: ✅ No `assert True` epidemic
props.content: Only one hit: tests/integration/pe/test_pe_adversarial_gates.py:228 — and it's a documented dormant-gate placeholder with explanatory message. Could be pytest.skip for clarity but acceptable. Whole rest of the suite is unusually clean on tautology.

props.title: ✅ `inject_transcript` is NOT a smell
props.content: It's a real production gate (gate.allow_transcript_injection). test_injection_only_start.py validates the daemon does NOT call create_backend when injection is enabled. Structural assertion, not a fake. (Correcting my own earlier pre-scan flag.)

props.title: ✅ No `mock_pipeline.parse` patterns
props.content: Searched the whole tree — no 'wrote test to its own fake output' style anywhere. The smell exists but it's concentrated in the 5 files above, not spread.

props.title: ✅ Integration lane is mock-free
props.content: 120+ active integration tests. Only 1 file (test_pipeline_e2e.py) has any mocks (2, both legit). Whatever cleanup ran recently scrubbed this lane very well. Don't undo that work.

props.label: PART 3
props.title: Your three concerns mapped to evidence
1. 'Faster tests' that lie
props.content: **CONFIRMED.** Unit lane is bimodal — top 5 files carry ~80% of mock load. Files #1 and #2 (test_intent_decision_gate, test_dispatch_engine) patch the production module their tests are supposed to validate. These are the candidates the previous agent over-mocked.

props.title: 2. 'demo' semantic drift
props.content: **CONFIRMED.** 5 distinct concepts under one word: integration test / scorecard tool / scorecard wrapper / profile guardrail / runtime stack. Same anti-pattern as the baseline-* proliferation pinned in CLAUDE.md.

props.title: 3. Hardcoded / over-mocking / fake values
props.content: **MOSTLY CLEAN** with 5 hot spots (above). The 'overboard' mocking is concentrated, not spread. Suite has no widespread tautology, no fake-pipeline patterns, and integration lane is essentially mock-free.

props.label: PART 4
props.title: Recommendations (priority order)
props.subtitle: What top test-engineering experts would do this week.

rows.why: Highest payoff. Real DispatchEngine wiring regressions silently pass today.
Decide whether mocks are at SUT boundary (OK) or inside it (rip out).
Dead lane, no recipe touches it, only adds context noise.

rows.action: Collapse Docker test lane → FakeDigiRig + recorded fixtures
rows.why: Tests shouldn't spin Docker. Helper already exists.
Stops the sprawl before it gets worse. CLAUDE.md already pins this discipline for baseline.

rows.action: Add fitness test: grep-ban patch('modules.*') in tests/unit with allowlist
rows.why: Prevents regression. One-line gate beats six months of vigilance.

props.title: 1. Fix the worst offender
props.content: Open tests/unit/intent/test_intent_decision_gate.py:490-523. If DispatchEngine is the SUT's collaborator, keep mock at boundary. If it IS production logic the test cares about, instantiate real. ~30 min.

props.title: 2. Fitness test for module-mocking
props.content: Grep-ban patch('modules\.') in tests/unit/ with explicit allowlist (logger, clock). One-time write, ongoing safety. 15 min.

props.title: 3. Delete the _archive lane
props.content: 13 files, no callers. git log to confirm last touch, then git rm. 10 min.

props.title: 4. Rename 'demo' recipes
props.content: demo-dump → score-turns. test-demo → test-demo-slice. gate-demo-mode-prod → gate-no-demo-mode-in-prod. 20 min.

props.title: ⬅️ Back
props.content: Return to PE compound-readback fix or voice-demo NATS work — both depend on these tests not lying.
